topics: 🕳️ Layered pit liability — first digger, last digger, plasterer
📏 Shiurim of a pit: 10 tefachim for death, anything for damage
🐑 What sancta count under מעילה — R' Yose haGlili, Ben Azzai, R' Shimon

overview.title: 📖 Overview — Pits, Layers, and Sancta
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: The two segments of daf 5 carry an enormous amount of weight. The first explores the Mishnah's clause הִכְשַׁרְתִּי בְּמִקְצָת נִזְקוֹ: when multiple people contribute to a single pit, who pays for what kind of damage? Rebbi and R' Yitzchak split the layers of liability between death and damage, and the Yerushalmi pushes outward to ask about plastering, dune-pits, and the minimum shiur. The second segment moves to property law — which sancta fall under מעילה for the purposes of our Mishnah's reference to נכסים? — and brings the layered debate of R' Yose haGlili, Ben Azzai, and R' Shimon.
overview.hebrewSource: כַּמָּה שִׁיעוּרָן לְהָמִית. עֲשָׂרָה טְפָחִים. וּלְהַזִּיק. כָּל שֶׁהוּא

slides.title: 🕳️ Layered Pit — Who Pays?
slides.subtitle: First digs ten, second deepens by one tefach.

nodes.content: חָפַר בּוֹר עֲשָׂרָה טְפָחִים וּבָא אַחֵר וְעָמַק בּוֹ טֶפַח
Beraita's plain rule: הָאַחֲרוֹן חַיָיב — the latter pays for everything
The pit is a stack of liabilities — different layers track different harms.

callout.label: Key move:
callout.text: הִכְשַׁרְתִּי בְּמִקְצָת נִזְקוֹ is not just a rhetorical clause. It is the legal mechanism for slicing one physical pit into multiple liability units.

slides.title: 💬 The Plasterer Case
slides.subtitle: Can someone become liable just by finishing another's pit?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: חָפַר בּוֹ עֲשָׂרָה טְפָחִים וּבָא אַחֵר וְסִיָידוֹ וְכִיָירוֹ שְׁנֵיהֶן חַיָיבִין — both are liable.

lines.speaker: Q
lines.text: Just by whitewashing and lining? That doesn't deepen the pit at all!

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: When the digger told him: סוֹד אֶת הַבּוֹר הַזֶּה וּקְנֵה אוֹתוֹ — finish it and own it. The plasterer becomes the owner.

lines.speaker: RI
lines.text: R' Immi b'shem R' Elazar: שְׁמִירַת נְזָקִין כִּשְׁמִירַת קִנְיָין — the same act that triggers ownership triggers liability.

lines.speaker: RS
lines.text: R' Simon: better — read it as digging in the dunes, where any new pit is a fresh hazard, so each new digger owns his stretch.

callout.label: Yerushalmi style:
callout.text: Two paths to make sense of the beraita: one through קנין principles, one through factual reframing. The Yerushalmi keeps both in tension.

slides.title: 📏 Shiurim of a Pit
slides.subtitle: How deep does it have to be?
rows: Any depth is enough to obligate for damage
Whatever fits the falling animal — chicken or camel

callout.label: Practical reading:
callout.text: The Yerushalmi gives three competing shiurim and lets them stand. R' Elazar haKappar's standard is brilliantly relative — the pit is sized to its victim.

slides.title: 🏛️ Sancta and מעילה — The Mishnah's Property Clause
slides.subtitle: What is included in the Mishnah's נְכָסִים שֶׁאֵין בָּהֶן מְעִילָה?

steps.content: 1️⃣ Beraita: הַנְּכָסִין הַלָּלוּ נִיקְנִין עִם נְכָסִין שֶׁיֵּשׁ בָּהֶן מְעִילָה.
2️⃣ Rav Yehudah b'shem Shmuel: this follows R' Yose haGlili.
3️⃣ R' Yose haGlili — וּמָעֲלָה מַעַל בַּיי includes קדשים קלים.
4️⃣ Ben Azzai narrows: שלמים only. Abba Yose ben Dosai: only בכור.
5️⃣ R' Shimon and Rav Huna split sancta by אחריות — if the owner bears risk, it's בָּעֲמִיתוֹ וְכִחֵשׁ; if not, בַּיי וְכִחֵשׁ.

callout.label: The pivot:
callout.text: What looks like a property-law footnote is really about classifying whose property the sacred animal is. אחריות — owner's risk — becomes the line that determines which clause of the מעילה verse applies.

slides.title: 🐑 The Three Tannaitic Positions on Sancta
slides.subtitle: Each Tanna draws the line differently.

boxes.title: 🐑 Ben Azzai
items: Per Abba Yose ben Dosai: only בְּכוֹר
שלמים implies מעשר too; מעשר excludes בכור

callout.label: Why this matters here:
callout.text: Bava Kamma 1:2 says the rule applies to נכסים. The Yerushalmi must define what counts as your property — and sancta sit on the boundary between mine and Hashem's.

summary.principle: The Mishnah's clauses about הִכְשַׁרְתִּי בְּמִקְצָת and נכסים unfold into a layered theory of pits and a careful sorting of which sancta still count as personal property.

flowSteps.content:
Pit case: 10 tefachim + 1 tefach
Beraita splits the liability

🎓 Key Principle:
שְׁמִירַת נְזָקִין כִּשְׁמִירַת קִנְיָין — the same acts that vest ownership vest liability. And the same property-test that defines מעילה defines whose נכסים the Mishnah is talking about.

quiz.q: If A digs a pit 10 tefachim and B deepens it by one, who is liable for damage according to R' Yitzchak?
o: Both A and B — אַחַר שְׁנֵיהֶן לִנְזָקִין
Neither, since each one is partial
quiz.e: R' Yitzchak reads the beraita: latter for death, but אַחַר שְׁנֵיהֶן לִנְזָקִין — both for damage.

quiz.q: How can a plasterer become liable for someone else's pit?
o: When the digger said סוֹד אֶת הַבּוֹר הַזֶּה וּקְנֵה אוֹתוֹ — plastering effects acquisition
Automatically, since he touched the pit
He never can — only digging counts
quiz.e: שְׁמִירַת נְזָקִין כִּשְׁמִירַת קִנְיָין: when plastering is the agreed קנין act, it makes him owner — and therefore liable.

quiz.q: What is the shiur of a pit לְהָמִית versus לְהַזִּיק?
quiz.e: The Yerushalmi states clearly: לְהָמִית עֲשָׂרָה טְפָחִים, וּלְהַזִּיק כָּל שֶׁהוּא.

quiz.q: What is R' Elazar haKappar's measure for a pit?
o: כִּמְלוֹאוֹ שֶׁלַּנּוֹפֵל — fitted to the falling animal, chicken or camel
quiz.e: R' Elazar haKappar's relative shiur: the pit is sized to whatever animal falls in.

quiz.q: What is R' Shimon's principle for which sancta count under 'against his neighbor and he lied'?
o: Sancta for which the owner bears אחריות
quiz.e: R' Shimon: when the owner is responsible for replacement (אחריות), the sancta are read as בָּעֲמִיתוֹ וְכִחֵשׁ; otherwise בַּיי וְכִחֵשׁ.
